Article Title: Nominee vs legal heir: What is the difference and who has claim on assets? All you need to know
Article Text:
AI image

Who is a Nominee?



Who is a Legal Heir?

Difference between a Nominee and a Legal heir

Aspect Nominee Legal Heir Role Custodian Owner Transfer of Assets Can be done without any legal formalities. Requires testamentary documents for proof of ownership. Powers No power to transact, use, or transfer assets. Power to use and transact assets as an owner. Appointment Owner can appoint anyone, but some institutions specify certain conditions. Determined by succession/personal laws. Legal Right Cannot contest ownership legally. Can contest ownership in a court of law.

When individuals open bank accounts, invest in mutual funds, or buy insurance, they are often asked to name a nominee.However, many people mistakenly assume that the nominee automatically inherits the assets. In reality, the nominee is not always the final recipient — the ownership rights belong to the legal heir, reported ET.Understanding the distinction between these two is essential to ensure that your wealth is passed on to the intended beneficiaries.A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets.The nominee’s role is limited to holding and managing the assets until they are legally transferred to the rightful heirs.According to ET, the nominee cannot sell, trade, or make any transactional decisions about the assets.The account or policy owner can appoint anyone — family, friends, or acquaintances — as a nominee, though financial institutions often prefer close family members.In some cases, the nominee and the legal heir can be the same person, which helps in ensuring a smoother transfer of assets.The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away.To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will or other testamentary proof.If there is no will — a situation known as dying intestate — the heir is determined as per the country’s succession or personal laws, reported ET.While a nominee can initially access the assets, only the legal heir can claim them permanently or challenge ownership in court.Experts quoted by ET emphasise that having clarity on this difference helps avoid disputes and ensures that the savings reach the intended family members or beneficiaries.Therefore, it is advisable to regularly update both nominees and wills to align with one’s estate planning goals.
Article Summary: A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets. The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away. To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will.

Article Title: Life Certificate 2025: Step-by-step guide to generate and submit Jeevan Pramaan online and offline
Article Text:
What is a Life Certificate and why does it matter



Annual submission deadline for pensioners in 2025



1 November and 30 November.

How to generate Jeevan Pramaan online: Step-by-step process



Step 1: Biometric device registration

Step 2: Authentication and certificate generation

Electronic delivery and access of Digital Life Certificate



How can you submit a Life Certificate in 2025



Offline submission at a bank branch or CSC centre

Online submission via Jeevan Pramaan Portal

Submission through the UMANG App

Who needs to submit the Life Certificate



Central and State Government pensioners

Defence pensioners

Family pensioners in the event of the pensioner’s death

Pensioners from public sector undertakings

Key details required before submitting the Life Certificate



Aadhaar Number

Registered Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

Pension Payment Order (PPO) Number

Bank Account Number where the pension is credited

Name of the Pension Sanctioning Authority

Is Aadhaar mandatory for a digital Life Certificate



Legal validity of Jeevan Pramaan



How Jeevan Pramaan simplifies pensioner verification



Every year, lakhs of pensioners across India must prove that they are still alive to continue receiving their pension. This verification, known as the Life Certificate or Digital Life Certificate (DLC), is a crucial and mandatory step in ensuring that pension payments are made only to eligible recipients, preventing fraud and misuse.Traditionally, this process involved visiting the bank or pension office in person, standing in long queues, and submitting a signed paper certificate. However, with the government’s focus on digitisation and technological advancement, this process has become much simpler, faster, and more convenient. Pensioners can now submit their certificates digitally from the comfort of their homes using Jeevan Pramaan, a secure, user-friendly, and Aadhaar-based platform.A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries.The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric-enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.Introduced by the Government of India, it uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication using either fingerprint or iris scan to verify a pensioner’s identity. Once verified, a digital certificate is automatically generated and shared with authorised pension disbursing agencies.This online process eliminates the need for physical verification, providing elderly pensioners with a simpler and more accessible way to maintain their pension benefits.According to PIB, The submission of the Life Certificate is mandatory for all pensioners who wish to continue receiving their pension without interruption. For the year 2025, the standard submission period is betweenTo make the process more comfortable for the elderly, the government allows pensioners aged 80 years and above to begin the submission process earlier, from 1 October 2025. This extended window gives senior citizens more time and flexibility to complete the procedure without facing last-minute difficulties.The Jeevan Pramaan process involves two main steps: registering the biometric device and generating the digital certificate.Before using the service, pensioners or operators must register their biometric device such as a fingerprint or iris scanner. During registration, the user provides their Aadhaar number and mobile number, after which an OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to their mobile. After entering the OTP, they must provide their name and email ID before scanning their fingerprint or iris.Once authentication is complete, the system confirms that device registration was successful, allowing the user to move to the next step.In this step, the pensioner enters personal details such as Aadhaar number, mobile number, name, PPO number, pension type, sanctioning authority, disbursing agency, and bank account details. The system also asks whether the pensioner has remarried or is re-employed.After entering all details, the pensioner scans their fingerprint or iris using the registered biometric device. Upon successful authentication, the Digital Life Certificate is generated, and an SMS acknowledgment with the Jeevan Pramaan Certificate ID is sent to the registered mobile number.This certificate is automatically stored in the Life Certificate Repository, accessible anytime to both pensioners and pension agencies.Once the Digital Life Certificate is created, it can be electronically delivered to the concerned Pension Disbursing Agency (PDA). PDAs can log in to the Jeevan Pramaan portal to view, verify, or download the certificate directly.Pensioners can also download a PDF copy of their certificate by visiting the Jeevan Pramaan website and entering their Pramaan ID or Aadhaar number. This ensures transparency and easy access for future reference.The government provides several options to submit the Life Certificate, allowing pensioners to choose the method that suits them best.Pensioners who prefer traditional methods can visit their bank branch or a Citizen Service Centre (CSC) to submit their Life Certificate physically. Trained staff at these centres assist pensioners in verifying their details and completing the process.For those unable to travel due to age or health issues, India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) offers a doorstep banking service. Pensioners can schedule an appointment, and an IPPB representative will visit their home to complete the submission using biometric authentication. This service is available for both IPPB and non-IPPB account holders.For pensioners comfortable with technology, the Jeevan Pramaan portal provides a completely online process. By visiting https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in, pensioners can submit their Digital Life Certificate using a computer, laptop, or smartphone equipped with a fingerprint or iris scanner.The system uses the Aadhaar platform to authenticate biometric details. Once verification is successful, a Digital Life Certificate is generated and stored in a secure online repository. Pension disbursing agencies can then directly access the certificate, removing the need for physical paperwork.The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app offers another easy option. Pensioners can download the app from the Google Play Store, search for “Jeevan Pramaan,” and select “Generate Life Certificate.”After entering the necessary details such as Aadhaar number, PPO number, and type of pension, pensioners must authenticate themselves using biometric verification. The app then generates a Digital Life Certificate (DLC), which is automatically shared with the pension disbursing authority.All pensioners who receive government, defence, or private sector pensions through authorised disbursing agencies are required to submit their Life Certificate every year. This includes:Failure to submit the certificate within the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of pension payments until verification is completed.To ensure a smooth submission process, pensioners should keep certain details and documents ready. These include:Having these details ready helps to avoid delays or errors during authentication, whether submitting online or offline.Yes, an Aadhaar number or Virtual ID (VID) is mandatory to generate a Digital Life Certificate. The entire Jeevan Pramaan system is based on Aadhaar authentication, which ensures accuracy and prevents fraud. Pensioners must make sure their Aadhaar is linked with their mobile number and bank account before attempting to generate the certificate.The Digital Life Certificate is fully valid and legally recognised under the Information Technology Act. Pension disbursing authorities across India accept it as an official document, equivalent to the traditional paper-based life certificate.Its biometric verification system makes it secure and tamper-proof, reducing the chances of wrongful pension disbursal or misuse of pension funds.Before the introduction of Jeevan Pramaan, pensioners often faced difficulties in submitting their certificates, especially those with health problems or limited mobility. The digital system has removed these challenges, allowing pensioners to submit their verification anytime and anywhere.By integrating biometric technology and Aadhaar authentication, Jeevan Pramaan has streamlined pension management, improving efficiency for both pensioners and government departments. It also enhances transparency and ensures that genuine beneficiaries receive payments on time.
Article Summary: A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries. The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric- enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.

Article Title: What are gold, silver hallmarking charges? Check details and importance
Article Text:
What is gold, silver hallmarking?



Hallmarking charges



ALSO READ |

Testing for purity



The importance of hallmarking



Old hallmarking signs



ALSO READ |

Hallmark symbols



HUID in the hallmarked gold jewellery



Key components of hallmark on gold jewellery



Gold, silver hallmarking : Gold and silver jewllery have long been treasured for their beauty and value. However, ensuring their purity and authenticity is important, especially in a market prone to fraudulent practices. This is where the hallmarking scheme comes into play. Here’s what you need to know about hallmarking charges and its procedures:Hallmarking is the process of accurately determining and officially recording the proportionate content of precious metals in articles, such as gold and silver.It serves as a guarantee of their purity or fineness. In India, hallmarking is governed by stringent standards outlined in IS 15820, according to an ET report.Hallmarking charges are applied per article, regardless of its weight. For gold articles, the charge is Rs 45 per article, with a minimum charge of Rs 200 for a consignment. Similarly, for silver articles, the charge is Rs 35 per article, with a minimum charge of Rs 150 for a consignment. It's important to note that additional taxes and levies may apply.A consignment refers to a collection of articles with the same fineness, from which a sample is drawn and assayed for compliance. Different lots may exist within a consignment.Anyone can have their hallmarked jewellery tested for purity at an A&H centre by paying a testing fee of Rs. 200 (for testing using the fire assay method as per IS 1418). These centres must be recognized by the BIS. The list of recognized A&H centres is available on the BIS website under the hallmarking section.The primary objective of hallmarking is to safeguard consumers from counterfeit products. To achieve this, the Indian government made hallmarking mandatory for gold jewellery from June 16, 2021. Since April 1, 2023, jewellers are prohibited from selling any gold jewellery without a 6-digit Hallmark Unique Identification ( HUID ) number.If you bought gold jewellery on or before June 30, 2021, it may have old hallmarking signs. These signs include the purity/fineness mark and the BIS emblem, along with two additional indications: the jeweller's identification number and the assay centre's mark/number.However, starting July 1, 2021, these two indications were replaced with a unique alphanumeric HUID code. The hallmark remains valid for the lifetime of the jewellery, and consumers can sell old jewellery, whether hallmarked or un-hallmarked, to jewellers.The HUID, or hallmark Unique Identification number, is a key component of hallmarking in gold jewellery. It's a unique six-digit alphanumeric code assigned to each hallmarked item, allowing for traceability. Customers can verify the HUID number using the 'Verify HUID' feature in the BIS Care App.Since the introduction of the HUID on July 1, 2021, the hallmark on gold jewellery consists of three marks: the BIS logo, the purity of the article in caratage and fineness, and a unique six-digit alphanumeric HUID number.
Article Summary: Hallmarking is the process of accurately determining and officially recording the proportionate content of precious metals in articles, such as gold and silver. In India, hallmarking is governed by stringent standards outlined in IS 15820. Since April 1, 2023, jewellers are prohibited from selling any gold jewellery without a 6-digit Hallmark Unique Identification number.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: Air India to cut an additional 145 weekly international flights
Article Text: NEW DELHI: International connectivity to and from India will see a sharp drop this summer with Air India set to suspend an additional about 145 weekly flights between this June and Aug. While these new cuts, which come on top of almost 100 daily flights being suspended earlier this summer, have been made to reduce costs amid spiralling operating expenditures and mounting losses, for passengers it will mean even higher fares on the truncated supply this summer as the big Gulf carriers are also operating a reduced capacity.Air India says it will try to make alternate arrangements for travel for affected passengers who had booked on flights that are now going to be cancelled, failing which a refund will be given.Region-wise, AI is cutting maximum flights to and from the east; followed by North America, Europe and Australia. While some international routes have been suspended temporarily, others will see frequency cuts.Routes like Delhi-Newark/Chicago/ Shanghai/Male; Mumbai New York (JFK)/Dhaka and Chennai-Singapore will be temporarily suspended. Delhi-Kathmandu frequency will be reduced from 42 times weekly to 28 weekly in June.
Article Summary: Air India set to suspend an additional about 145 weekly flights between this June and Aug. Region-wise, AI is cutting maximum flights to and from the east; followed by North America, Europe and Australia. Delhi-Kathmandu frequency will be reduced from 42 times weekly to 28 weekly in June.

Article Title: Princeton University scraps its more than 100-year-old 'Honor Code' for exams; Dean says in letter: A significant number of undergraduate students ...
Article Text:
Princeton University scraps its more than 100-year-old 'Honor Code' for exams;

Here’s why Princeton University is scrapping its 100-year-old code



New exam rules at Princeton University







Princeton University has now ended its more than a century-old tradition of unproctored exams, citing growing fears of cheating fuelled by artificial intelligence. According to a report by The Wall Street Journal, the faculty at Princeton University voted this week to require proctoring in all in-person exams starting this summer. This new change will reverse a policy that had been in place since 1893. For almost 133 years Princeton relied on its Honor Code, which enabled the students to take exams without the presence of any professor or invigilator. The students at the university also pledged not to cheat and the violations were reported to a student-run honor committee. The code was considered to be a cornerstone of Princeton’s culture and a point of pride.As per the WSJ report, in a letter to the students, Dean of the College Michael Gordin said “a significant number of undergraduate students and faculty” requested the change, given perceptions that cheating has become widespread. He further noted that the presence of AI tools has made it easier for the students to cheat and it has also made it harder for the faculty to detect. Also, the students are increasingly reluctant to report peers for fear of social media backlash.As part of new policy, the instructors will be present during the exams and are also required to document and report any infractions to the honor committee. Despite the nee policy, the students will still have to sign the pledge which reads, “I pledge my honor that I have not violated the Honor Code during this examination”. But proctoring will now act as an added safeguard.Nadia Makuc, a senior who chaired the honor committee also said that most of the strands at the university support the new change as it reduces the pressure to report the classmates. The WSJ report adds that the committee handled around 60 cases last year, but she believes many violations went unreported. “If the exam is on a laptop, someone can just flip to another window. Or if it’s in a blue book, people use their phone under the desk,” she explained.
Article Summary: The faculty at Princeton University voted this week to require proctoring in all in-person exams starting this summer. This new change will reverse a policy that had been in place since 1893. For almost 133 years Princeton relied on its Honor Code, which enabled the students to take exams without the presence of any professor or invigilator.

Article Title: Goldman Sachs CIO Marco Argenti may have just told so many IT companies that you are doing AI wrong by telling your employees to ...
Article Text:
How AI changed the way employees work at Goldman Sachs





Goldman Sachs tech boss Marco Argenti recently said that companies may be focusing on the wrong thing by measuring how employees use artificial intelligence (AI) at work. While many firms are pushing workers to use AI tools at work more often, Argenti believes that tracking employee’s AI usage is the wrong way to measure whether it is making them more productive. Instead, companies should focus on the speed at which a team can develop a future, he said. “It would be like looking at only one player on the field,” Argenti said in a Business Insider interview adding “Fine, this player is doing more movements, but why am I not scoring more goals? Well, because they need to pass the ball.”Explaining further, he stated that this measuring approach helps as one can see a productive team’s work backlog starts shrinking rapidly.During the interview, Marco Argenti explained how AI has changed the way employees work at Goldman Sachs. Instead of spending long periods creating presentations to explain ideas, teams can now quickly build working prototypes and improve them instantly based on feedback.“There’s zero time between idea and prototype. You kind of ‘3D print’ software,” he said.The chief information officer also revealed that the investment bank is taking a different approach to adopt AI technology.He revealed that Goldman Sachs measures how quickly its engineering teams can move from coming up with an idea to actually executing on it.Argenti further revealed that the Goldman Sachs staff has moved away from the previous fearfulness or skepticism about AI, adding that employees ‘attitudes are starting to change’. “The dominant sentiment is really a sense of empowerment. People feel almost liberated. A few weeks or months ago, of course, there was a real bit of skepticism and fear, but I correlate that to people that were not really using it,” he said in the interview.Goldman Sachs has been investing heavily in AI tools in recent years. In 2024, the bank launched its GS AI Platform, which uses large language models from companies including OpenAI and Google with added security layers to protect company data.The bank has also introduced internal AI systems similar to ChatGPT and a research tool called “Legend,” which helps employees search through company files using natural language.
Article Summary: Goldman Sachs tech boss Marco Argenti said companies may be focusing on the wrong thing by measuring how employees use artificial intelligence (AI) at work. Argenti believes that tracking employee’s AI usage is the wrong way to measure whether it is making them more productive. Instead, companies should focus on the speed at which a team can develop a future.

Article Title: Google rolling out AI-powered ‘Contextual suggestions’ on Android: Here’s what it is
Article Text:
Privacy and security



Disable location tracking for suggestions.

Delete stored data at any time.

Manage preferences under Settings > [Your name] > All services > Other > Contextual suggestions.

Manage contextual suggestions



Before you use contextual suggestions for the first time, you’ll find a notice about the feature that provides a link to your settings.

If you cast audio or video from your device:

You must have Android 14 or up.



You can find the notice when you start an active media session while connected to the Wi-Fi network and cast to a device.

On your Android device, open the Settings app. Tap Apps All apps Google Play services. Tap All services Developer features Contextual Suggestions. Turn Use contextual suggestions on or off. Under "Data used for your suggestions:” To use contextual suggestions without location data, turn off Use device location.

When you turn off “Use device location,” contextual suggestions remain on. Your location data won’t be used to give the recommendations.



When "Use device location" is on, location data can only be used if your device location is on.

To manually delete all stored data:

Tap Manage your data.



Next to “Delete all stored data,” tap Delete



To confirm deletion, in the pop-up box, tap Delete.





Google has announced the rollout of Contextual suggestions on Android. This is a new AI-powered feature which is designed to learn from user habits and offer timely recommendations directly on device. Google introduced the feature with the Pixel 10 series running Android 16. The Contextual suggestions feature uses AI to analyse the device activity and location data to predict what might be useful at a given time. For instance, the system can suggest playlists when you’re at the gym or prompt you to cast sports games to your TV at the usual time. These recommendations are designed to make everyday smartphone use more intuitive and personalised.The feature is rolling out on Pixel 10 and Pixel 10a devices with Android 16 and Google Play services version 26.18. It is not yet available on older Pixel models or Android 17 Beta releases.Google emphasises that contextual suggestions operate entirely within a secure, encrypted space on the device. Data is never shared with apps or Google unless the user explicitly grants permission. Apps can act on predictions to offer suggestions but cannot access the underlying data.Users retain full control with options to:Important:Tip: The data used for contextual suggestions is automatically deleted after 60 days. You can also delete it at any time before that.
Article Summary: Google has announced the rollout of Contextual suggestions on Android. This is a new AI-powered feature which is designed to learn from user habits. Google introduced the feature with the Pixel 10 series running Android 16. It is not yet available on older Pixel models or Android 17 Beta releases.

Article Title: Hack of the day: Auto-translate YouTube captions
Article Text:
'The Times of India' brings you 'Hack of the Day'—a new weekday series of quick, practical solutions to everyday hassles. Each hack is designed to save you time, money or stress, using tools and features within your reach—from government websites to everyday apps. In simple terms, it's simple fixes for smarter living.'

What it solves



How to use it



Open the YouTube video you want to watch.

Tap settings or captions to check if captions are available.

Turn on captions in the original language.

Choose auto-translate if your language isn’t listed.

Select your preferred language to read subtitles while watching.

Note of caution







One of the most powerful ways to learn online is through video, but language barriers often get in the way. YouTube’s auto-translate captions feature helps break those barriers by allowing you to read subtitles in your preferred language, even when the video doesn’t offer them directly. This means you can access lectures from international universities, follow recipes from chefs abroad, understand tutorials from creators in other countries, or watch product reviews and explainers without missing key details. Instead of limiting yourself to content in your native language, auto-translate captions open up a global library of knowledge, making YouTube a truly universal classroom.With auto-translate, you don’t have to skip valuable content because it’s in another language. It instantly converts available subtitles into a language you understand, letting you learn from creators worldwide.This hack is great for lectures, recipes, tutorials, product reviews, and explainers. However, accuracy depends on the original audio and captions, so translations may not always capture every nuance. Still, it’s a powerful way to expand your learning across languages.
Article Summary: YouTube’s auto-translate captions feature allows you to read subtitles in your preferred language. It instantly converts available subtitles into a language you understand, letting you learn from creators worldwide. This hack is great for lectures, recipes, tutorials, product reviews and explainers.

Article Title: Canon EOS R6 V with RF 20-50 F4L IS USM lens launched: Price in India, features about the company’s latest camera for video makers
Article Text:
Canon EOS R6 V with RF 20-50 F4L IS USM lens price in India



Product Price in India (MRP) EOS R6 V (Body Only) Rs 2,35,995 RF 20-50 F4L IS USM Lens Rs 1,42,995 EOS R6 V + RF 20-50 F4L Bundle Rs 3,55,995

Canon EOS R6V camera launch: ‘From India to the world’



EOS R6 V: Made for filmmakers



Canon brings the Canon’s first professional Power Zoom lens



Key Specifications at a Glance:



Sensor: 32.5MP Full-Frame CMOS.

32.5MP Full-Frame CMOS. Video: 7K Open Gate RAW/MP4; Canon Log 2/3 and Custom LUT support.

7K Open Gate RAW/MP4; Canon Log 2/3 and Custom LUT support. Stabilization: Up to 8.0 stops of Coordinated Control IS (In-Body + Lens).

Up to 8.0 stops of Coordinated Control IS (In-Body + Lens). Lens Range: 20mm (ultra-wide) to 50mm (natural perspective).

20mm (ultra-wide) to 50mm (natural perspective). Aperture: Constant f/4 throughout the zoom range.

Constant f/4 throughout the zoom range. Audio: 4-channel audio recording and a full-size HDMI Type-A terminal.





Canon has launched its latest camera, the Canon EOS R6V camera the RF 20-50 F4L IS USM Lens in India, targeting video creators. This is the company’s first-ever full-frame professional-grade L-series lens with a built-in power zoom. Positioned as a “future-ready” ecosystem, the new duo is designed to meet the high-speed demands of wedding filmmakers, regional cinema and digital-first content creators – combining cinematic imaging, advanced video performance, mobility and lens innovation in one ecosystem.Canon specifically highlighted the importance of India and growing creator community in the country. According to Tiger Ishii, managing executive officer, Canon, president, Canon Marketing Asia, President & CEO, Canon Singapore Pte Ltd, India is one of the most strategic markets worldwide standing at the forefront of a powerful storytelling evolution.“From weddings, cinematography and regional cinema to digital-first content and independent productions, creators today demand tools that deliver cinematic excellence, mobility, and workflow agility without compromise,” he said.Toshiaki Nomura, President and CEO, Canon India, said: “With the launch of the EOS R6V camera and RF 20-50 F4L IS USM lens we are strengthening Canon’s video-focused ecosystem in India with a future-ready solution built for modern filmmaking and creator-led storytelling.”Canon says that the EOS R6 V has been heavily optimised for video, and takes cues from the EOS R6 Mark III. The camera features a 32.5MP full-frame sensor capable of 7K Open Gate recording – a feature that allows creators to records using the entire sensor area, which essentially making it easier to crop the footage into both horizontal and vertical formats without losing image quality.One of the highlights of the camera is the inclusion of a fan that addresses the common “overheating” issue of modern mirrorless cameras. The Canon EOS R6 V includes a built-in cooling fan, allowing for recording times up to three times longer than its predecessors, the company claims, adding that the camera gets built-in optical image stabilisation to cancel out the vibration caused by the fan movement.When it comes to user interface, Canon says that it automatically rotates when shooting vertically, catering to the “Reels and Shorts” era. The camera gets a dedicated vertical tripod socket.Pairing with the new body is the RF 20-50 F4L IS USM, Canon’s first-ever professional-grade L-series lens to feature a built-in Power Zoom – designed for smooth, cinematic transitions. Users can control the zoom via a ring on the lens or a lever on the camera body, with 15 adjustable speed levels.The features addresses the camera balance shift issue because the lens barrel does not change length while zooming, allowing it to be used on gimbals or complex rigs.The Canon EOS R6 V features a front-facing recording button and a "tally lamp" that glows red when the camera is live, allowing to “selfie” content creation . The camera also supports the new Wireless Remote Controller K544, which comes bundled with a portable tripod grip that doubles as a selfie stick.
Article Summary: Canon has launched the EOS R6V camera and RF 20-50 F4L IS USM lens in India. This is the company’s first-ever full-frame professional-grade L-series lens with a built-in power zoom. The camera features a 32.5MP full- frame sensor capable of 7K Open Gate recording.

Article Title: As GM cuts hundreds of tech jobs; laid off employees say while 'describing job terminations': Oddly timed virtual meeting, scripted message from …
Article Text:
“No appreciation or empathy. No questions. Nothing.”

What laid-off employees said about how GM conducted the job cut process



“They’re going to push AI for everyday work and everything else. I’ve seen it firsthand. It can make you much more productive as a programmer. It can really help you get more work done, but AI isn’t going to do you any good if you don’t know the business.”

“GM is transforming its Information Technology organisation to better position the company for the future.”

“skills swap,”





General Motors recently announced it's laying off hundreds of its technology team members. Now, employees affected by the automaker’s latest round of job cuts have reportedly claimed that the company ended their employment with an unexpected 15-minute virtual meeting, a pre-written message from human resources, and an abrupt disconnection.According to a CNBC report, affected GM employees described receiving calendar invites for meetings at unusual times, only to find themselves on short virtual calls where their roles were terminated with little interaction. Describing the experience, a data analyst who worked for more than a decade at GM told the publication,This comes after the company announced it is laying off between 500 and 600 salaried employees, primarily from its information technology division across locations, including Austin, Texas, and Warren, Michigan. The cuts represent more than 10% of GM’s IT workforce and come as the company reshapes its teams around artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities.Two employees familiar with the process told CNBC that the meetings followed a consistent pattern: a brief introduction, a scripted statement from HR, and immediate termination of access. Workers said there was no opportunity to ask questions or seek clarification during the calls.The layoffs come amid internal restructuring, with some employees noting their teams had already undergone recent changes. Several said they had been encouraged in recent months to incorporate artificial intelligence tools into their daily workflows.A veteran programmer and data scientist at GM told CNBC,While the job cuts may appear to be cost-driven, they align with a broader shift in hiring strategy. GM is actively recruiting talent focused on AI-native development, data engineering, and cloud-based infrastructure, indicating a transition away from traditional IT roles.In a statement, the company said,This is what industry watchers refer to as aas companies fill roles tied to legacy systems with those who build and deploy AI systems in-house. Businesses are looking for employees who can build models, manage data pipelines, and design AI-driven workflows, rather than relying on third-party tools.
Article Summary: GM recently announced it's laying off between 500 and 600 salaried employees, primarily from its information technology division. The cuts represent more than 10% of GM’s IT workforce and come as the company reshapes its teams around artificial intelligence (AI) capabilities.

Article Title: Samsung announces One UI 9 beta program for Galaxy S26 series phones
Article Text:
Samsung One UI 9 beta features

Samsung Notes gains creative new tools, including decorative tapes and a wider variety of pen line styles. Meanwhile, the Contacts app now offers direct access to Creative Studio for creating personalized profile cards without the need to switch between apps.



The updated Quick Panel gives users greater control over its layout. Brightness, sound and media player are each independently adjustable, with more size options to suit individual preferences.



Accessibility is significantly improved by an adjustable Mouse Key speed that enables smoother cursor control and a combined TalkBack package that brings together features previously offered separately by Google and Samsung. The new Text Spotlight feature — which displays selected text larger or more clearly in a floating window — has also been added to make reading easier.



One UI 9 also introduces enhanced protection against suspicious apps and potential threats: when new high-risk apps are detected, it now warns users, blocks execution and installation, and recommends deletion through security policy updates.





One UI 9 beta availability





Samsung has announced the One UI 9 beta program for Galaxy S26 series. Rolling out later this week, One UI 9 beta is built on Android 17. It is claimed to deliver expanded creative tools, customization options, a more accessible mobile experience and stronger protection against potential security threats. Samsung said that the full experience of One UI 9 will be introduced with upcoming Galaxy flagship devices later this year, which will include advanced AI features that will make mobile interaction easy and effortless.One UI 9 beta introduces several updates across key areas of the mobile experience.The One UI 9 beta program will be available to Galaxy S26 series users in select markets, including Germany, India, Korea, Poland, the U.K. and the U.S starting this week. Galaxy S26 series users can apply to join the beta program via the Samsung Members app.
Article Summary: Samsung has announced the One UI 9 beta program for Galaxy S26 series. Rolling out later this week, One UI9 beta is built on Android 17. It is claimed to deliver expanded creative tools, customization options, a more accessible mobile experience and stronger protection against potential threats.

Article Title: Samsung opens registration for Vision AI TVs in India ahead of May launch
Article Text: Samsung has announced that customers in India can now register their interest for its upcoming 2026 Vision AI televisions, set to launch later this month. The new lineup promises advanced features and cutting-edge technology designed to deliver an enhanced viewing experience. In 2026, Samsung will expand its Vision AI Companion (VAC) across all 4K UHD and above models. This move reflects the company’s broader strategy to make AI a central part of the TV experience, bringing AI-powered viewing to more categories, screen sizes, and consumer needs.Samsung, which has held the title of Global No. 1 TV brand for 20 consecutive years, continues to focus on premium and ultra-large screen innovation. The Vision AI televisions are the latest step in its push to integrate AI into everyday entertainment.Customers can register between May 12 and May 25 on Samsung’s Hand-raiser page hosted on Samsung.com or at retail outlets. Those who register will be eligible for an early bird discount of up to Rs 10,000 on selected products once sales begin.Samsung launches AI-powered Mini LED TVs in IndiaRecently, Samsung launched its latest Mini LED TV lineup. Starting at Rs 42,990, the new range spans screen sizes from 43-inch to 100-inch, making premium display technology accessible to a wider audience.The new Mini LED TVs feature Samsung’s MetalStream Design, inspired by aircraft aesthetics. With a single-metal body and ultra-slim bezels, the TVs deliver an immersive edge-to-edge viewing experience. The design reflects Samsung’s focus on combining elegance with durability, ensuring the TVs stand out in modern living spaces.The new Mini LED TVs are available across leading retail outlets and online platforms, including Flipkart and Samsung.com. Customers can benefit from launch offers such as up to 5% cashback and zero down-payment EMIs with flexible tenures of up to 30 months.At the core of the launch is Samsung’s Mini LED technology, which uses LEDs half the size of conventional ones for precise light control. This enables deeper contrast, enhanced clarity, and life-like visuals. Features such as Pure Spectrum Color, Color Booster, and Mini LED HDR ensure pixel-level accuracy and vivid colour reproduction, covering over 90% wide colour gamut.The TVs are powered by the NQ4 AI Gen2 Processor, which uses a 20-neural-network architecture for real-time picture and sound optimisation. This includes superior upscaling, enhanced contrast control, and improved clarity across content types. An AI Sound Controller adapts audio performance to user preferences, while Motion Xcelerator 144Hz ensures smooth motion for gamers and sports fans.
Article Summary: Samsung has announced that customers in India can now register their interest for its upcoming 2026 Vision AI televisions. The new lineup promises advanced features and cutting-edge technology designed to deliver an enhanced viewing experience. In 2026, Samsung will expand its Vision AI Companion (VAC) across all 4K UHD and above models.
